entries.description: Post-2016 coup purge: Over 150,000 people dismissed from jobs, 80,000+ arrested, including judges, teachers, journalists, and academics under broad 'terrorism' charges
entries.causation_evidence: Amnesty International, Human Rights Watch, and Council of Europe reports

entries.description: Systematic crackdown on independent media: Turkey ranks among the worst jailers of journalists in the world for over a decade; multiple news outlets seized or shut down
entries.causation_evidence: Reporters Without Borders World Press Freedom Index (2014–2025)

entries.description: Erosion of judicial independence: Widespread reports of politically motivated prosecutions, including high-profile cases against opposition figures and critics
entries.causation_evidence: European Commission Turkey Report and Venice Commission opinions

entries.description: Military operation in northeast Syria and subsequent displacement of hundreds of thousands of civilians
entries.causation_evidence: UN Human Rights Council reports on Operation Peace Spring

entries.description: Ongoing imprisonment of political opponents, including former HDP leaders and journalists, under anti-terrorism laws
entries.causation_evidence: Amnesty International and HRW 2025 Turkey reports

entries.description: Constitutional referendum expanding presidential powers amid widespread allegations of voter intimidation and irregularities
Significant economic growth and infrastructure development over multiple decades, including major projects in transportation and energy
entries.causation_evidence: World Bank and Turkish government economic reports

entries.description: Long-standing NATO membership and role in regional security, including hosting major military installations
